subject: Structure fire — 412 Oak St (electrical)
fromName: Bellwood Fire Dept · Shift Cmdr. Alvarez
snippet: Incident BFD-2026-3344: structure fire at 412 Oak St. Engine 41 and Ambulance 9 responded. One occupant treated for smoke inhalation, refused transport. Cause: electrical, basement panel.
body:
INCIDENT REPORT — BFD-2026-3344
Type: Structure fire
Location: 412 Oak St
Units: Engine 41, Ambulance 9
Dispatched: 03:18  On scene: 03:24  Cleared: 05:02

Narrative: Units arrived to find smoke showing from the basement of a single-family home. Fire was contained to the electrical panel and surrounding framing. One adult occupant was evaluated for smoke inhalation and refused transport. Cause determined to be a failed basement electrical panel. ComEd notified to disconnect service. Owner referred to code enforcement for re-inspection before re-occupancy.

Injuries: 1 minor (refused transport). Fatalities: 0. Estimated loss: $18,000.

subject: Storm sewer inspection — Eastern Ave & 19th (recurring flooding)
snippet: Camera inspection of the Eastern Ave storm main found 40% blockage from root intrusion between 19th and 20th. This is the likely cause of the repeat basement flooding reported by residents this spring.
body:
STORM SEWER INSPECTION REPORT
Segment: Eastern Ave storm main, 19th to 20th
Method: CCTV camera
Date: 2026-06-22

Findings: Approximately 40% cross-sectional blockage from root intrusion and accumulated debris at two joints. Flow restricted during moderate rain events. Consistent with the pattern of basement backups reported by residents on Eastern Ave and 19th this spring.

Recommendation: Hydro-jet and root-cut this segment within 30 days; schedule a follow-up camera run. Estimated cost $6,500. A capital fix (spot lining) recommended in the next budget cycle.

subject: Theft from vehicle — 1500 block St Charles Rd
snippet: Case BPD-2026-20871: theft from a parked vehicle on the 1500 block of St Charles Rd overnight. No forced entry; report taken, referred to investigations. Third such report on that block this month.
body:
POLICE REPORT — BPD-2026-20871
Offense: Theft from motor vehicle
Location: 1500 block St Charles Rd
Reported: 2026-06-23 09:05
Officer: Ofc. Reyes #412

Narrative: Complainant reported items taken from an unlocked vehicle overnight. No forced entry. Report taken and referred to investigations. Note: this is the third theft-from-vehicle report on this block in June; investigations is reviewing for a pattern. Crime-prevention flyer recommended for the block.

subject: Building permit BP-2026-742 — deck addition, 233 25th Ave
snippet: Permit BP-2026-742 issued for a rear deck addition at 233 25th Ave. Applicant Westside Builders LLC. Footing inspection passed; final inspection pending.
body:
BUILDING PERMIT — BP-2026-742
Property: 233 25th Ave
Applicant: Westside Builders LLC
Work: Rear deck addition, 12x16, pressure-treated
Status: Issued — footing inspection passed 2026-06-20; final pending
Inspector: C. Patel

Notes: Setbacks verified against the survey. No variance required. Final inspection to confirm railing height and stair rise before occupancy of the structure.

subject: Code case CE-2026-318 — standing water complaint, 88 Marshall Ave
snippet: Resident complaint of chronic standing water in the rear yard at 88 Marshall Ave after rain. Inspection found a collapsed downspout drain tying into the village storm system. Notice to repair issued.
body:
CODE ENFORCEMENT CASE — CE-2026-318
Property: 88 Marshall Ave
Complaint: Standing water / mosquito breeding after rain events
Inspection: 2026-06-21, Officer Flores

Findings: Rear-yard downspout drain has collapsed where it ties into the village storm lateral, causing ponding. Related to the broader Eastern Ave drainage issue. Notice to repair issued to the owner; 30 days to correct. Public Works notified of the storm-lateral connection.

subject: Village Board minutes — June 16, 2026 (flooding mitigation discussion)
snippet: Minutes of the June 16 regular meeting. The Board discussed recurring flooding on Eastern Ave and directed Public Works to report on storm-sewer capacity and bring a capital estimate to the July budget workshop.
body:
VILLAGE OF BELLWOOD — BOARD MEETING MINUTES
Regular meeting, June 16, 2026

Item 6 — Stormwater / flooding. Several residents spoke during public comment about repeated basement flooding on Eastern Ave and the surrounding blocks. The Board directed Public Works to (a) complete storm-sewer inspections on the affected segments, (b) report findings, and (c) bring a capital cost estimate for sewer improvements to the July budget workshop. Motion carried 6-0.

Item 7 — Fire Department quarterly report accepted. Item 8 — Permits update. Meeting adjourned 8:42 PM.

subject: EMS run — medical assist, Bellwood Senior Apartments
snippet: Incident BFD-2026-3361: medical assist at Bellwood Senior Apartments. Patient evaluated and transported to Loyola in stable condition. Building elevator out of service noted for facilities follow-up.
body:
INCIDENT REPORT — BFD-2026-3361
Type: EMS — medical assist
Location: Bellwood Senior Apartments, 200 Bellwood Ave
Units: Ambulance 9 (Medic 9)
Dispatched: 19:47

Narrative: Responded for an elderly resident with difficulty breathing. Patient assessed, stabilized, and transported to Loyola in stable condition. Crew noted the building's main elevator was out of service, complicating the carry-down; recommend the village follow up with facilities management re: elevator reliability for emergency access.

subject: Pavement & catch-basin survey — 25th Ave corridor
snippet: Survey of the 25th Ave corridor rated pavement fair-to-poor and flagged two catch basins with failing grates. Recommended for inclusion in the FY27 road program alongside drainage work.
body:
PAVEMENT & DRAINAGE SURVEY — 25th Ave corridor
Date: 2026-06-19

Pavement condition index: fair-to-poor over the southern three blocks; surface cracking and two depressions noted. Catch basins: two with failing/ sunken grates that pond water and contribute to localized flooding near 25th & Washington.

Recommendation: bundle resurfacing with catch-basin replacement; include in the FY27 road program. Coordinate with the Eastern Ave storm-sewer work for efficiency.

subject: FOIA 2026-114 — records on Eastern Ave flooding response
snippet: FOIA request for all records — inspections, work orders, complaints, and Board discussion — relating to the village's response to recurring flooding on Eastern Ave over the past 12 months. Response due in 5 business days.
body:
FREEDOM OF INFORMATION ACT REQUEST — 2026-114
Requester: news media
Received: 2026-06-26

Request: All records concerning the Village's response to recurring flooding on Eastern Ave and adjacent blocks in the past 12 months, including storm-sewer inspection reports, work orders, resident complaints, code cases, and any Board discussion or minutes.

Status: Open. Statutory response due within 5 business days. Clerk compiling responsive records across Public Works, Code Enforcement, and Board minutes.
